warnings: This sheet (A-13) presents 3 door types (D1, D2, D3, circle symbols) across the top row and 5 window types (W1-W5, hexagon symbols) below/beside them -- same total count (3 doors, 5 windows) as sister houses' equivalent sheet.

Nearly all dimensions and material specs (aluminum frame/white powder coat, 6mm/4mm glass, wood door 2x4 frame, PVC bathroom door, W5's stair-landing note) are IDENTICAL to house #4's equivalent A-14 sheet -- strongly cross-confirms both readings, consistent with the shared government housing-program template. The one exception is W3: this house's W3 is 1.40 x 2.30 (1.80+0.50 height split) vs house #4's 1.40 x 2.50 (2.00+0.50) -- a genuine 0.20m per-house difference, not a misread default.

D/W numbering re-derived by position/type since the raw sheet's own per-cell symbol numbering restarts independently for doors (1,2,3) vs windows (1,2,3,4,5) and would collide if used verbatim as element_id -- same convention used for sister houses' equivalent sheet.

W5 lights the ST.3 main switchback stairwell per its '▼ระดับชานพักบันได' note, consistent with the floor plans (หน้า06/07) showing โถง (containing the ST3 stair) as the central room, and รูปด้าน 4 (หน้า12)'s window '5' appearing directly above the central ground-floor window group.
This sheet is A-13 (not A-14 as in house #4) since this house has only 2 sections (A-11, A-12) instead of 3, shifting every later sheet number down by 1.
